description_long: This object is a vintage-style radio receiver with a rectangular wooden body and a dark front panel. The front face features a large circular speaker grille on the left, flanked by three rotary knobs and dials for tuning and volume control, all set in a metallic or plastic bezel. A prominent, slender antenna extends vertically from the top left corner, suggesting FM/AM radio functionality. The sides and back are plain, with the wood grain texture visible throughout, and the back panel appears solid with no visible openings or controls. The overall design is compact and portable, reminiscent of mid-20th-century radios. The materials and finish give it a classic, durable appearance suitable for both indoor and outdoor urban environments.
description: A compact, vintage wooden radio receiver with a front speaker grille, three control knobs, and a tall antenna. The design is classic and functional, with a rich wood texture and metallic accents.
description_view_0: The front view shows the speaker grille, three control knobs, and the base of the antenna, highlighting the main interactive features.
description_view_1: The left side view displays the plain wooden side and the antenna extending upward, with minimal visible features.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a solid, unadorned wooden back panel with the antenna emerging from the top left corner.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the plain wooden surface and the antenna, with no controls or speaker visible.
